content.problemStatement:
Chef has a network of computerCountcomputerCountcomputerCount computers numbered from 000 to computerCount−1computerCount - 1computerCount−1, connected by ethernet cables represented as cableConnectionscableConnectionscableConnections. Each cable connection links two distinct computers directly. 

Chef wants to ensure that every computer in the network can communicate with every other computer, either directly or indirectly through other computers. To achieve this, Chef can disconnect cables between directly connected computers and reconnect them between pairs of disconnected computers. Help Chef find the minimum number of such reconnections needed to connect all computers. If it is impossible to connect all computers, return −1-1−1.

Function DeclarationFunction NameminOperationsToConnectNetworkminOperationsToConnectNetworkminOperationsToConnectNetwork - This function calculates the minimum number of cable reconnections required to connect all computers in a network.

Parameters
computerCountcomputerCountcomputerCount : The total number of computers in the network.
cableConnectionscableConnectionscableConnections : A list of existing cable connections, where each connection is represented as a pair `[computerA, computerB]`.
Each connection indicates a direct link between two distinct computers.
Return Value
Returns a number.
The return value represents the minimum number of operations needed to ensure all computers are connected.
If it is impossible to connect all computers, the function returns `-1`.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤computerCount≤2×1051 \leq computerCount \leq 2 \times 10^51≤computerCount≤2×105
0≤connectionCount≤min⁡(computerCount×(computerCount−1)2,105)0 \leq connectionCount \leq \min\left(\frac{computerCount \times (computerCount - 1)}{2}, 10^5\right)0≤connectionCount≤min(2computerCount×(computerCount−1)​,105)
0≤computerA,computerB<computerCount0 \leq computerA, computerB < computerCount0≤computerA,computerB<computerCount
computerA≠computerBcomputerA \neq computerBcomputerA=computerB
There are no repeated connections.
No two computers are connected by more than one cable.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of:

The first line contains an integer computerCountcomputerCountcomputerCount — the number of computers in the network.
The second line contains an integer connectionCountconnectionCountconnectionCount — the number of existing cable connections.
The following connectionCountconnectionCountconnectionCount lines each contain two integers computerAcomputerAcomputerA and computerBcomputerBcomputerB — representing a cable connecting these two computers.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum number of cable reconnections required to connect all computers in the network.
If it is impossible to connect all computers, print `-1`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of: The first line contains an integer computerCount, the number of computers in the network. The second line contains an integer connectionCount, the number of existing cable connections. The following connectionCount lines each contain two integers computerA and computerB, representing a cable connecting these two computers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum number of cable reconnections required to connect all computers in the network. If it is impossible to connect all computers, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there are 6 nodes and 6 connections. The network has two components: one with nodes {0,1,2,3} and another with {4,5}. One extra cable can be moved to connect these two components, so the output is 1. For the second test case, all 8 nodes are already connected in a single network, so no operations are needed.
There are 4 nodes and 3 connections. The network is already connected, so no operations are needed.
There are 5 nodes and 4 connections. The network is already connected, so no operations are needed.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the Union-Find algorithm. We first initialize the parent array and the rank array. Then, we iterate over each connection and union the two computers. After that, we count the number of connected components. If there is only one connected component, we return 0. Otherwise, we return the number of connected components minus 1 if the number of connections is greater than or equal to the number of computers minus 1. Otherwise, we return -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (computerCount + connectionCount * alpha(computerCount))) per test case, where alpha is the inverse Ackermann function. The total time complexity is O(T * (computerCount + connectionCount * alpha(computerCount))).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(computerCount) — the dominant structure is the parent array and the rank array.
